title: Hash passwords with a slow, memory-hard KDF
description: Store password verifiers with Argon2/scrypt/bcrypt and a per-user salt, never with a fast or unsalted hash.
body: Use when storing or verifying user passwords. Do not use MD5/SHA-1/SHA-256 directly for passwords, and avoid a global or missing salt. Actions: pick a memory-hard KDF (Argon2id preferred, else scrypt or bcrypt), set parameters (memory, iterations, parallelism) to the highest the workload tolerates, generate a unique CSPRNG salt per user, and store the algorithm and parameters alongside the hash so they can be upgraded. Verify with the library's constant-time comparison. Validation signals include a test that the stored verifier is salted, memory-hard, and rejects a wrong password. Risk boundary: a fast or unsalted password hash makes offline cracking and rainbow tables trivial after a breach; treat it as a high-severity finding requiring rehash-on-login migration.
